g.text: Where Overlaat fits in a self-hosted LLM stack
Overlaat does NOT route or load models. It is a fair-queue + honest-accounting ring around an off-the-shelf serving stack.
OFF-THE-SHELF — what it sits in front of (gateway, backends, swap layer)
OFF-THE-SHELF serving stack — Overlaat sits in front of all of this
Clients · interactive chat · bursty batch · agents
• per-model FIFO semaphore — overflow WAITS, never a 429
• slot size derived from the gateway config, not tuned here
• emits exactly ONE lifecycle event per request
model aliases · virtual keys · routing. Bound to loopback so the proxy is the only door —
no second entry, no accounting hole.
SERVING — off-the-shelf backends the gateway routes to (two kinds)
each process loads its own model(s) on demand —
no external orchestration, all can be resident together
Overlaat just queues + counts these. Each backend
a GROUP MUTEX for big models that can't co-reside:
only ONE is resident — a sibling request evicts + cold-loads
A SEPARATE off-the-shelf component, NOT part of Overlaat.
Overlaat only observes its cold loads (→ model_loads).
request_events one row per request lifecycle
host_samples host GPU% · RAM · per-backend RSS
read-only dashboard — never writes the live path
honest concurrency — three curves, defined once:
EVIDENCE — one request_events row (real field names)
t_acquire: 1718800002.340, // NULL if cancelled while queued
prompt_tokens: 1840, completion_tokens: 512 // NULL if no usage
Takeaway: Overlaat queues, accounts, and observes. The gateway routes; the swap layer loads and evicts.
